title: Collection elements should not be replaced unconditionally
description: Collection elements should not be replaced unconditionallyAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?Storing a value inside a collection at a given key or index and then unconditionally overwriting it without reading the initial value is a case of a "dead store".list[index] = "value 1"; list[index] = "value 2"; // Noncompliant dictionary.Add(key, "value 1"); dictionary[key] = "value 2"; // NoncompliantThis practice is redundant and will cause confusion for the reader. More importantly, it is often an error and not what the developer intended to do.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights res
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Collection elements should not be replaced unconditionally. This may indicate a dead store or logic error.
